scope.summary: Implement primitive panel shape constructors (Rectangle, Circle/Ellipse, Polygon) that emit a closed EdgeShape outline into GarmentSpec as a new Panel
acceptance_criteria: author_panel_rect(width_cm, height_cm, corner_radius_cm) produces a Panel with 4 Line EdgeShapes (or Bezier corners when radius>0) meeting at right-angles, closed outline verified by PANEL_CLOSED descriptor
author_panel_circle(radius_cm) produces a Panel with a single Bezier-approximate closed ellipse EdgeShape
author_panel_polygon(vertices: Vec<[f32;2]>) produces a Panel with N Line EdgeShape segments and a closed outline
All three primitives emit TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded; GarmentSpec spec_json updated in tailor_panels row
PANEL_CLOSED ValidationDescriptor passes for every constructed primitive
Model-accessible via the edit_panel MCP tool with a shape_preset field (rect/circle/polygon) and dimension params
scope.risk_if_missed: Models and operators have no way to start a garment from scratch; every GarmentSpec must be authored as raw JSON with no tooling aid. The starter template library (MT-137) partially compensates but only for the 5 seeded archetypes. Any novel shape requires hand-writing coordinate arrays, which is error-prone and blocks the model-first authoring path for non-template garments.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
